question: Will SPD win the second most seats in the 2026 Sachsen-Anhalt parliamentary elections?
description:
Parliamentary elections to elect the Landtag of Sachsen-Anhalt are scheduled to take place on September 6, 2026.

This market will resolve to the political party that wins the second greatest number of seats in the state parliament of Sachsen-Anhalt (Landtag) as a result of this election.

If voting in the Sachsen-Anhalt election for the Landtag does not occur by January 31, 2027, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid second votes received (Zweitstimme), with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the second finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as published on the official website of the Landtag of Sachsen-Anhalt (https://www.landtag.sachsen-anhalt.de/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus strongly favors CDU for second place in Sachsen-Anhalt's Landtagswahl, with 88% implied probability reflecting consistent voter surveys showing AfD leading at 30-35% and CDU at 25-29%. Recent INSA and Forsa polls from late 2024 confirm this gap, as AfD maintains dominance amid eastern Germany's migration and economic discontent, while CDU benefits from stable support under state leader Reiner Haseloff. BSW's rise to around 15% poses a distant challenge but hasn't closed on CDU, with SPD, Die Linke, Greens, and FDP mired below 10%. No major shifts from campaign events or national trends have altered this positioning ahead of the 2026 vote.

question: Spread: Gainare Tottori (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for April 5 at 1:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Gainare Tottori" if Gainare Tottori win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Reilac Shiga".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on jleague.jp. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Gainare Tottori vs. Reilac Shiga - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for April 5 at 1:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Reilac Shiga's superior recent form drives trader consensus toward them in Gainare Tottori vs. Reilac Shiga markets, with the visitors unbeaten in their last five J3 League matches including three wins, while Tottori has won just once in six, exposing defensive frailties conceding 10 goals. Tottori hosts at Matsuyama Central Park, leveraging home advantage where they've earned 60% of points this season, but Shiga's attacking momentum under coach Naoto Otake—averaging 1.8 goals per game lately—poses a threat. No major injuries reported from official team updates, though Tottori's key midfielder Takayuki Arakaki remains doubtful per beat reports. Upcoming schedule favors Shiga with rest edge post-midweek cup tie, tilting implied probabilities amid J3's tight standings battle.

question: Will Ukraine re-enter Obratne or Temyrivka by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Ukraine captures any territory of Obratne (47.827331° N, 36.548744° E) or Temyrivka (47.825114° N, 36.570672° E), Zaporizhzhya Oblast, between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Ukrainian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Reported Ukrainian Territorial Gains in the Past 24 Hours", or “Claimed Ukrainian Counteroffensives” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Ukraine comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded blue in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Ukraine de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces' confirmed advances in Donetsk Oblast have solidified control over villages like Otradne and Temyrivka, recently captured amid Ukraine's strained defenses near Pokrovsk, driving the 92.5% "No" odds on Ukrainian re-entry by March 31. Official Russian Ministry of Defense statements and geolocated footage verify these gains, with no verified Ukrainian counteroffensives reclaiming the areas despite localized probing attacks. Ukrainian military updates highlight resource shortages, harsh winter weather, and prioritized defenses elsewhere, reducing prospects for rapid recapture. Trader consensus reflects this momentum, tempered by historical base rates of stalled winter fronts in the conflict, with upcoming aid packages unlikely to shift dynamics before the deadline.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors "No" at 90.5% for Ukraine re-entering Maliivka by March 31, driven by entrenched Russian defensive lines in Zaporizhzhia Oblast and the stalled Ukrainian counteroffensive since mid-2023. Recent developments, including Russia's consolidation of positions around Robotyne and Verbove after Ukrainian gains last summer, show no momentum toward Maliivka, a small village under occupation since early 2022. Harsh winter conditions, delayed Western aid deliveries, and Kyiv's focus on Donetsk frontlines like Avdiivka further limit operational capacity. Official Ukrainian military updates report incremental advances elsewhere but no specific Maliivka operations, reinforcing skepticism about meeting the tight deadline amid static southern frontlines.

question: Will Designer Brands Inc (DBI) beat quarterly earnings?
description:
As of market creation, Designer Brands Inc is estimated to release earnings on March 26, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Designer Brands Inc’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $-0.51 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Designer Brands Inc reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $-0.51 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Designer Brands Inc releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 63.5% implied probability for Designer Brands Inc (DBI) to beat Q3 FY2025 earnings estimates, primarily driven by the company's track record of four consecutive quarterly EPS beats, including a 20% surprise in Q2 on June 5. Supporting this sentiment, DBI's adjusted EBITDA margin expanded to 7.2% amid cost controls, despite -4.5% comparable sales reflecting consumer spending caution in discretionary retail. Forward-looking pressures include softening footwear demand and high inventory levels, with consensus projecting $0.27 EPS on $693 million revenue. Key catalyst: September 5 earnings release, where gross margin trends above 32% could validate upside, though macroeconomic headwinds cap enthusiasm below 70%.

Recent developments bolstering sentiment include a 25% stock rally over the past month on robust June sales data and analyst upgrades citing 25%+ revenue growth projections, though tempered by competitive pressures from Douyin and margin compression risks. Key catalyst: Earnings release on August 26, with consensus EPS at $2.78 and revenue at $10.2 billion—thresholds PDD has historically cleared with room to spare.

question: Will any country expel a U.S. ambassador by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any U.S. ambassador is expelled from their assigned country by the government of that country between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59: PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Any expulsion from a country where a U.S. ambassador is assigned as of the time of this market’s creation will qualify.

For the purposes of resolving this market, an official announcement that a U.S. ambassador will be or is being expelled will suffice regardless of whether or not the respective ambassador leaves the country within this market’s timeframe.

Qualifying announcements must be definitive, unambiguous, official statements issued by an authorized governmental person or entity.

Whether the ambassador resigns, is recalled, or otherwise departs voluntarily will have no bearing on the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United States, including the U.S. Department of State, as well as official information from the relevant expelling government; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus at 98.6% for "No" stems from the rarity of diplomatic expulsions, which typically require severe bilateral crises like espionage claims or military escalations—none of which currently threaten U.S. ambassadors worldwide. Recent developments show no official announcements, protests, or retaliatory moves from nations with tense relations, such as Russia, Iran, or Venezuela, despite ongoing global frictions in Ukraine and the Middle East. Historical precedents, like Russia's 2021 action, involved explicit provocations absent today. Confidence remains high with the March 31 deadline approaching, though unforeseen intelligence leaks or sudden geopolitical shocks could prompt a late reversal.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects near-certainty that no European country will expel a U.S. ambassador by March 31, driven by the absence of any diplomatic crises, espionage allegations, or official rebukes from EU or NATO member states' foreign ministries. U.S.-Europe relations remain stable amid policy frictions over Ukraine aid and trade, but far from the rupture required for such a rare, escalatory move—last seen in non-allied contexts decades ago. Recent silence from primary sources like government statements reinforces this view, with the short timeline leaving little room for surprises. Realistic shifts could stem from abrupt bilateral incidents, such as verified intelligence disputes, though evidence points overwhelmingly against them.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no European country expelling an Israeli ambassador by March 31, driven by the absence of any official diplomatic ruptures despite heightened tensions over the Israel-Gaza conflict. Recent actions, such as Spain, Ireland, and Norway recognizing Palestine and summoning Israeli envoys for protests, have stopped short of declaring ambassadors persona non grata—a rare step reserved for severe escalations like proven war crimes or direct hostilities. Strong EU-Israel trade ties, NATO alignments, and U.S. influence further deter such moves. Realistic shifts could arise from an adverse ICJ ruling, major Hezbollah escalation, or unified EU pressure, but with the deadline nearing and no momentum building, traders see negligible risk.

question: Will Steve Cohen be the Democratic Nominee for TN-09?
description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the TN-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on August 6, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

question: Will USD fall to 1.4M Iranian rials by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the finalized USD exchange rate on Bonbast is equal to or below the specified price for any day between market creation and March 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve according to the daily finalized free-market USD exchange rate as displayed on Bonbast (https://www.bonbast.com/graph/usd), which publishes prices in Iranian toman, where 1 Iranian toman equals 10 Iranian rials (IRR).

A daily figure will be considered finalized once the following day’s figure is released.

Revisions or corrections to daily figures indicating a qualifying exchange rate will be considered only if they occur before all relevant figures for this market have been finalized.

The resolution source for this market will be Bonbast (https://www.bonbast.com/graph/usd). Resolution will occur once the March 31, 2026, exchange rate data is finalized. If the resolution source becomes permanently unavailable, another resolution source will be chosen.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for the USD reaching elevated levels against the Iranian rial by March 31 centers on accelerating depreciation driven by sustained US sanctions and regional conflicts. The black market exchange rate recently hit record highs above 71,000 rials per dollar following Israel's October strikes on Iranian facilities, which disrupted oil revenues and spiked domestic inflation past 40%. Limited central bank reserves hinder interventions, while anticipation of tighter "maximum pressure" policies post-US inauguration in January heightens volatility. Traders weigh upcoming Iranian budget debates and any nuclear negotiation signals against historical patterns of 20-30% annual weakening, embodying skin-in-the-game consensus on limited rial stabilization prospects.

question: Will there be at least 1700 measles cases in the U.S. by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there have been the specified amount or more confirmed cases of Measles (Rubeola) in humans in the territory of the United States of America in 2026, according to the CDC case counter by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter 'Total Cases' in 2026 (see: https://www.cdc.gov/measles/data-research/index.html) at the resolution time. If the counter becomes unavailable, another credible source will be used.

Note: Only cases reported by the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter will qualify, regardless of reports from U.S. State agencies or other sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for U.S. measles cases by March 31 hinges on a sharp early-2024 surge, with CDC data showing 102 confirmed cases across 16 jurisdictions as of March 28—more than double 2023's total and the highest start since 2019's nationwide outbreak. Most stem from Chicago's Pilsen shelter outbreak among unvaccinated migrants, spilling into schools, amplified by measles' R0 of 12-18 in under-vaccinated pockets below 95% MMR coverage. Historical baselines show cases often cluster post-importation; models suggest potential for 150+ if exposures persist. Key watch: CDC's April 5 weekly update finalizing March 31 tally, plus state health alerts on containment.

question: Spread: FC Augsburg (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Bundesliga game, scheduled for March 22 at 2:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Augsburg" if FC Augsburg win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "VfB Stuttgart".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on bundesliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Augsburg vs. VfB Stuttgart - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Bundesliga game, scheduled for March 22 at 2:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: VfB Stuttgart holds strong trader consensus as favorites heading into their Bundesliga matchup at FC Augsburg, fueled by four consecutive wins and top-four standing, while Augsburg languishes in 14th with one victory in seven games. Stuttgart's attack, led by Deniz Undav's seven goals, exploits Augsburg's leaky defense conceding 1.7 goals per game at home. Official reports list Augsburg without key forward Samuel Essende (hamstring), weakening their counter-threat, though Stuttgart misses suspended Enzo Millot. Recent form shows Stuttgart unbeaten in five away league games; head-to-head tilts 3-2 to Stuttgart lately. Upcoming schedule pressures Augsburg amid relegation fight, tilting odds toward Stuttgart's 58% implied win probability.

In the upcoming Bundesliga game between FC Augsburg and VfB Stuttgart, scheduled for March 22 at 2:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if FC Augsburg and VfB Stuttgart combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on bundesliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Augsburg vs. VfB Stuttgart - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Bundesliga game, scheduled for March 22 at 2:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: VfB Stuttgart holds strong trader consensus as favorites heading into their Bundesliga matchup at FC Augsburg, fueled by four consecutive wins and top-four standing, while Augsburg languishes in 14th with one victory in seven games. Stuttgart's attack, led by Deniz Undav's seven goals, exploits Augsburg's leaky defense conceding 1.7 goals per game at home. Official reports list Augsburg without key forward Samuel Essende (hamstring), weakening their counter-threat, though Stuttgart misses suspended Enzo Millot. Recent form shows Stuttgart unbeaten in five away league games; head-to-head tilts 3-2 to Stuttgart lately. Upcoming schedule pressures Augsburg amid relegation fight, tilting odds toward Stuttgart's 58% implied win probability.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

events.title: AS Roma vs. US Lecce - More Markets

This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between Misa Esports and New Project in the United21 Group B, initially scheduled for March 11 at 7:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Misa Esports" if Misa Esports win the match against New Project.

This market will resolve to "New Project" if New Project win the match against Misa Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: New Project holds a commanding edge in trader sentiment for their United21 Group B BO3 clash with Misa Esports, fueled by a hot streak of four series wins in five matches, including dominant 2-0 sweeps on Inferno and Nuke. Misa Esports, mired in a 1-5 skid, grapples with roster flux after parting ways with AWPer "shalfey" mid-tournament, weakening their CT-side holds. Head-to-head history tilts 3-1 to New Project, with superior pistol round wins (62% vs. 41%). Markets imply 72% probability for New Project, factoring group stage pressure for playoff seeding; Misa's upset path hinges on Mirage T-side aggression, but fatigue from back-to-back losses looms large.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. events.title: Hull City AFC vs. Coventry City FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the EFL Championship game, scheduled for April 6 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hull City's home advantage at the MKM Stadium anchors trader consensus for a tight Championship matchup against Coventry City, with implied probabilities reflecting both sides' playoff aspirations amid middling form. Hull enter off a gritty 2-1 win over Portsmouth, boosting momentum under Tim Walter, but face defensive concerns without suspended captain Jacob Greaves and injured Alfie Jones. Coventry, coming from a 1-1 draw at Middlesbrough, boast a solid away record (W3 D2 L2) yet grapple with striker Ellis Simms' hamstring doubt and Haji Wright's inconsistency. Recent head-to-heads favor Coventry (3 wins in last 5), but Hull's rest edge after midweek could shift dynamics in this October 19 clash.
